Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant C. Herb Berger, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant C. Herb Berger, Inc.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Nicole Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-5896. Email: nrodich@sftc.org.

Jason Rose, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Rosalyn Johnston and Roderick Johnston, Jr., who was placed under oath and testified.
Counsel and Mr. & Mrs. Johnston all appeared via CourtCall this morning.
No appearance by defendant C. Herb Berger, Inc.
Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint for Personal Injury-Loss of Consortium was filed on October 26, 2018.
Default as to defendant C. Herb Berger, Inc. was entered on July 10, 2019.
Statements of Damages were filed on July 10, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs Rosalyn Johnston and Roderick Johnston, Jr., and against the defaulted defendant C. Herb Berger, Inc., as follows:

Economic Damages as to plaintiff Rosalyn Johnston - $416,665.00.
Non-Economic Damages as to plaintiff Rosalyn Johnston - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Roderick Johnston, Jr. - $2,000,000.00.

Total - $12,416,665.00.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sarah Bendon of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-493-3563), appearing for plaintiffs Rosalyn Johnston, et al.
Evanthia M. Spanos of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Daniel E. Trowbridge of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant Resco Holdings LLC.
Jonathan Theonugraha of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8061), appearing for defendant Crane Co. via Court Call.
Jennifer C. Rasmussen of DEMLER, ARMSTRONG & ROWLAND, LLP (415-949-1900), appearing for defendant Familian Corp. via Court Call.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' Motion to Maintain the Current Trial Date with Preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The October 21, 2019 trial date is continued to January 13, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is January 24, 2020.

All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the October 21, 2019 trial date. Expert discovery to track new trial date. Fact discovery remains open for the limited purpose of acquiring medical records and social security disability records, pursuant to local rules. All other fact discovery remains closed. Medical records includes radiology and pathology.

Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 6, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than December 20, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by January 3, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, January 9, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. in 603.

Order signed.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439). (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs Rosalyn Johnston and Roderick Johnston Jr.
D. Mark Jackson of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4455), appearing for defendant Resco Holdings LLC

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted:

DEFENDANT RESCO HOLDINGS L.L.C. (PREVIOUSLY SUED AS DOE 302)'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT:

Defendant Resco Holdings LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Plaintiff Rosalyn Johnston was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) That Plaintiff Roderick Johnston's co-workers Thomas O'Mahoney, Timothy O'Mahoney, and Leonard Williams lack personal knowledge regarding the asbestos content of gaskets, packing, and valves they testified to using is insufficient to show that Plaintiff cannot obtain evidence of exposure. (see Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2007) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1439.) Defendant does not address Plaintiff's interrogatory response regarding the specified brands of materials at issue, stating that various defendants to General Order 29 and/or 129 Interrogatories to All Defendants indicate that asbestos containing gaskets or packing were manufactured during the relevant time frame. (Trowbridge Decl. Ex. F at 11.)

Order signed. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of Aiken Welch, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580). (503/CML)
